blurb: macOS includes an application-level firewall controlling incoming network connections on a per-application basis, independent of port or protocol. Signed App Store applications are automatically trusted; other applications prompt the user for allow/deny authorization when first attempting to accept incoming connections. A stealth mode option prevents the Mac from responding to network probe requests from scanners. macOS also provides access to the pf packet filter for low-level network traffic filtering and port blocking, configurable via command line or third-party firewall applications for advanced network policies in enterprise deployments.
